channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Top Dems, activists call on Biden admin to dole out more student loan forgiveness before term ends
item.description: Top Democratic lawmakers and activists alike are calling on the Biden administration to ignore a federal injunction and continue wiping out student loan debt before its tenure comes to a close.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Trump campaign lawyer tries to invalidate his guilty plea in Georgia election case
item.description: Former Trump campaign lawyer Kenneth Chesebro, who pleaded guilty to a conspiracy charge in the Georgia 2020 election meddling case against the president-elect and others, is trying to invalidate that plea.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is locked in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said said he is “not that worried” about Musk’s influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump announces picks for Army secretary, trade adviser, hostage envoy, NASA administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced several new additions to his incoming administration on Wednesday, including his selections for Army Secretary and NASA adminstrator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump transition signs MOU with DOJ to facilitate FBI background checks
item.description: President-elect Trump’s transition team announced Tuesday it had signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with the Justice Department, which will allow the FBI to conduct background checks for certain individuals chosen for the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis ordered to turn over communications in Trump case to conservative group
item.description: A Georgia judge ordered Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to turn over to a conservative watchdog group any communications she may have with special counsel Jack Smith or the now-disbanded House Jan. 6 committee.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's defense pick Pete Hegseth faces deepening scrutiny in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Defense Secretary, spent a second day on Capitol Hill, meeting privately with Republican senators amid rising questions about his ability to effectively lead the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DNC chair candidate: Democrats must be willing to 'recenter' agenda
item.description: Ken Martin, chair of the Minnesota Democratic-Farm-Labor Party (DFL) and a candidate for the Democratic National Committee (DNC) chair, argued the party must be willing to “recenter” its agenda after it suffered a string of defeats during last month’s elections.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions have scored a major legal victory with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden isn't the first president to pardon a relative. Here's how the power works
item.description: Biden is now the third president to pardon a relative, after Bill Clinton and Donald Trump. Here's a look at the commonplace — yet often controversial — presidential power.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: White House says Trump wouldn't 'let go' of Hunter Biden in defending pardon
item.description: The White House on Monday defended President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter Biden after insisting for more than a year that he wouldn’t do so.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump celebrates Thanksgiving alongside Elon Musk, family in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump celebrated Turkey Day alongside his family, and other notable figures in South Florida this year.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: Among those targeted were New York Rep. Elise Stefanik, Trump's pick to serve as ambassador to the United Nations, and Matt Gaetz, Trump's initial pick to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
